comment_text:
Based on her actions across various media, Wednesday Addams could be sued for several intentional torts, which are civil wrongs that cause harm to another person. While her "crimes" are typically treated as black comedy in the fictional world, the victims could seek damages in a court of law. 
Intentional torts from the Netflix series Wednesday
Battery
Battery is the intentional and unpermitted touching of another person. In the first episode, Wednesday releases a bag of piranhas into the school's swimming pool, resulting in a bully being hospitalized and losing a testicle. While she intended to hurt the group of boys harassing her brother, the most immediate claim would come from the injured student for battery and the resulting severe physical injuries. 
Assault
While battery requires physical contact, assault is the intentional creation of a reasonable apprehension of imminent harmful or offensive contact. By releasing the piranhas, Wednesday caused a foreseeable panic among all the students in the pool, placing each of them in fear of being attacked. 
False imprisonment
False imprisonment is the intentional confinement or restraint of another person's movement without justification. After discovering the Hyde monster's identity, Wednesday repeatedly tortures Tyler by tying him up and forcing him to transform, confining him against his will. While she was ultimately proven right, her actions would not be legally justified. 
Intentional infliction of emotional distress (IIED)
IIED is a tort that occurs when one acts outrageously and intentionally to cause severe emotional distress to another. Wednesday's extreme and alarming behavior could be used to sue her for IIED, particularly by characters like Enid, whom she repeatedly mistreats and verbally attacks. 
Trespass to land and chattel
Trespass involves an unauthorized intrusion upon the property of another.
Trespass to land: She breaks into the Jericho morgue to examine a monster victim.
Trespass to chattel: The medical examiner could sue for the unauthorized and intrusive autopsy and illegal copying of government files. 
Intentional torts from Addams Family Values
Battery and assault
In this film, Wednesday and Pugsley attempt to murder their new baby brother, Pubert. A baby sitter could sue Wednesday for multiple counts of assault and battery for various dangerous pranks, including attempts to throw the baby off the roof, electrocute him, and decapitate him with a guillotine. 
False imprisonment
During her time at Camp Chippewa, Wednesday and her friends are held against their will in a cabin for refusing to participate in camp activities. If the camp counselors were involved, they could sue Wednesday for false imprisonment if she prevented them from leaving the cabin. 
Other possible claims
Negligence: If the camp counselors could prove Wednesday's pranks were not intentional but showed a lack of reasonable care, they could sue for negligence. This could cover a variety of less direct harms.
Conversion: The unauthorized and permanent taking of the medical files from the morgue could also constitute the tort of conversion, which is the civil version of theft.

The Gospel summed up... 


1. We have all sinned and fallen short of God's glory...


• 1 sin disqualifies us from heaven 


• 1 sin separates us from God 


• 1 sin is enough to send us to Helll 



2. God came down to earth, in human form (Jesus)...


• To live the perfect life for us (from His birth, up until His death & resurrection), perfectly upholding all of God's law and having a perfect record, without a trace of sin (something we could never do)


• To suffer for us, leading up to the cross and on the cross


• To die for us



3. He did this...


• to take the FULL punishment and sentence, for all of ours sins (a punishment and sentence, that we deserve and not Him)


• Because His loves us and does not want any of us, to die in our sins, end up in hell and be eternally separated from Him 



4. This means, if we...


• Truly believe and follow Jesus


• Confess our sins to God and ask for His forgiveness 


• Repent and turn away from, what God considers sinful and evil


• Forgive others of their sins (as God forgives us of our sins)

• Forgive us of our sins

video_title: Wednesday: Season 2 | Part 2 Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
While a decent enough show, I  find it to be....how should I say it... too sterile. Like being in a theme park. Plus, too many characters who add absolutely nothing.

Great interesting characters: Wednesday of course; Enid; Fester...not enough of him; Gomez; Thing; and yes, that psycho tour guide from Pilgrim  World.  She lights up the screen. Oh...and also the pigtail stalker kid...she great too.
Those who are completely dull throwaways: Morticia; Pugsley;  The Siren and her Mom; the bee kid (this seasons version); Enid's new boyfriend; the new sheriff; and pretty much everyone else.
But the one who is utterly horrible and detracts immensely is the over the top wacky principal, ironically played by one of the greatest actors of our generation Steve Buscemi. What a waste of incredible talent (see Fargo, Big Labowski, Sopranos, Reservoir Dogs, Boardwalk Empire....plus many more) Just awful. Guess it was an easy paycheck....oh and, yes, the same old tired  cartoony Tim Burton monster template that he has used in absolutely everything from the beginning of his filmography. I wonder how great this would have been in the hands of the late great David Lynch ...but then, I guess kids couldn't watch!

comment_text:
Please, please write a better script. I had so much hope and waited such a long time to watch this, but I can point out a few disappointing parts:

The ending focused on the personal story of Wednesday’s mom and dad, but what did they actually do? → Nothing. They were just like props in the background during the final fight. (Look at how Harry Potter handled the scene with Lupin, Sirius, Snape, and Pettigrew in the Shrieking Shack — every character there had a purpose and something meaningful to contribute.)

Mr. Addams met his best friend but didn’t even get a single decent line, and then was pushed aside just to avoid cluttering the frame before the big battle. Mrs. Addams, who taught Wednesday everything, ended up just running around aimlessly in the fight…
